practicals.title: Practical 1: Introduction to Database Creation
practicals.desc: Setup and create a database using MySQL or PostgreSQL. Design entities such as Student, Course, Instructor, and Enrollment with relationships like student-course enrollment and instructor-course mapping.

practicals.title: Practical 2: Views and Indexing
practicals.desc: Create views for reporting student and course data. Perform update and delete operations using views and apply indexing to improve query performance.

practicals.title: Practical 3: SQL Queries with Filters and Sorting
practicals.desc: Use SQL queries with WHERE, ORDER BY, GROUP BY, and nested subqueries to filter, sort, and analyze student course data.

practicals.title: Practical 4: Multi-Table Joins
practicals.desc: Develop a customer order management system and use INNER JOIN, LEFT JOIN, RIGHT JOIN, and FULL OUTER JOIN to retrieve related data.

practicals.title: Practical 5: PL/SQL Procedures and Functions
practicals.desc: Design a sales management system using stored procedures and functions to insert data and calculate total sales.

practicals.title: Practical 6: Triggers and Cursors
practicals.desc: Develop an employee management system using triggers for audit logging and cursors to process salary updates based on conditions.

practicals.title: Practical 7: MongoDB Aggregation and Indexing
practicals.desc: Perform CRUD operations in MongoDB, use aggregation queries for analysis, and apply indexing to improve performance.

practicals.title: Practical 8: Social Media Data Processing using NoSQL
practicals.desc: Import and analyze social media data using MongoDB. Perform queries to extract insights such as trending hashtags.

practicals.title: Practical 9: MySQL Database Backup and Recovery
practicals.desc: Learn and implement database backup and recovery techniques in MySQL to ensure data security and disaster recovery.
